title: Garage Sale

jokes.text: You're eating eight-year-old tomatoes. They're still good for another week.
jokes.setup: Dwight explains his survival shelter food rotation system
jokes.punchline: Casually eating nearly decade-old food like it's normal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight character moment - his survival obsession leads to absurd logic about food safety

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I have a complete rotation system. Every six months, I consume the oldest item and replace it with fresh supplies. It's perfectly safe.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Dwight, that can is from 2015.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Exactly. Peak freshness window.

jokes.text: I've got some cheese you might like too... In between my toes.
jokes.setup: Offering cheese like a normal friendly gesture
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's toe cheese
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Creed gross-out humor with perfect delivery structure

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: Want some cheese?

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: It's toe cheese.

jokes.text: Kevin will be eaten. Pam will be taken slave. Jim will be made a warlord's Jester. Meredith will do okay.
jokes.setup: Dwight describing apocalyptic fates for coworkers
jokes.punchline: Meredith will do okay - implying she's already living rough
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect character-specific punchline about Meredith's lifestyle

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Meredith will do okay.

jokes.text: Be assured this day will come. It's just a matter of time. Could be one month, could be two months. Three months. - Could be. - Four months.
jokes.punchline: Jim playing along with increasingly mundane timeframes
jokes.explanation: Good Jim-Dwight dynamic with Jim enabling Dwight's absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: The end times are upon us. I estimate we have approximately 72 hours before civilization collapses.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: 72 hours? That's pretty specific. What happens at hour 73?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Complete anarchy. Society will crumble.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Okay, so should I finish this report by then, or...?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Jim, this is serious.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I know, I just want to know if I should hit send on this email to accounting before the apocalypse.

jokes.text: 494 months. I could see that happening. 495 months. That's just...
jokes.setup: Timeline has escalated to ridiculous lengths
jokes.punchline: Jim finally breaks at 495 months (over 40 years)
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the escalation - Jim's breaking point is funny

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Okay, Dwight, I'm done. 495 months? That's over 40 years. You've won. I concede.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Finally! I knew you didn't have the stamina for a true Schrute-level commitment.

jokes.text: I really thought I was becoming too much of a womanizer. I realized I had shirts in five different women's houses.
jokes.setup: Packer worried about being too much of a womanizer
jokes.punchline: His evidence is having shirts at multiple women's houses
jokes.explanation: Typical Packer sleazy character moment with good structure

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: I'm worried I'm becoming too much of a womanizer. I've got shirts at like five different women's houses.

jokes.text: Truth is, I got a couple love bumps on my ding-dong, so I was like, 'oh, game over.'
jokes.setup: Explaining why he's changing his ways
jokes.punchline: Crude euphemism for STDs
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that fits Packer's character but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: I got tired of collecting all those merit badges down below, if you know what I mean.

jokes.text: It was the best of times. It was the 'awesomest' of times.
jokes.punchline: Adding made-up word 'awesomest' shows his ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Packer's pseudo-intellectual attempts

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: It was the best of times, it was the awesomest of times.

jokes.text: Whoa, I'm sorry. Michael, I thought we were meeting Holly today, not Jennifer Aniston.
jokes.setup: Packer meeting Holly for first time
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top flirting comparing her to Jennifer Aniston
jokes.explanation: Predictable sleazy pickup line but fits character perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: Holly, you are absolutely stunning. You look just like Jennifer Aniston.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: Oh, thank you!

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: No, I mean you're even more beautiful than Jennifer Aniston. If Jennifer Aniston and Angelina Jolie had a baby, that baby would be jealous of you.

jokes.text: Just so you know, he's at his funniest when you've given him, like, five shots. And it also helps if you've had five shots. I already have.
jokes.setup: Michael giving Holly advice about Packer's humor
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's already drunk
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Michael's typical oversharing

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Holly, listen to me. You can't let Packer's jokes get to you. You just have to laugh along, even if they're not funny. It's about being a team player.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: But Michael, some of his jokes are really offensive.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I know, I know. But that's just how he is. You gotta roll with it. In fact, I've had like five beers and I think his jokes are hilarious right now.

jokes.text: Ladies and gentlemen, I would like to introduce you to a man who needs no introduction, probably because most of you have done it with him.
jokes.punchline: Twist on 'needs no introduction' meaning they've slept with him
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on common phrase with good delivery structure

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Everyone, please welcome our guest speaker. And, uh, he needs no introduction.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Mostly because he's already been introduced to everyone in this office. Personally. Very personally.

jokes.text: Just kidding. He wouldn't be interested in any of you.
jokes.punchline: Insults entire office as not attractive enough
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - trying to be funny but being cruel instead

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know what? None of you are attractive enough anyway. I mean, look at this office. It's like a bad episode of 'The Ugly Olympics.'

jokes.text: It's great to be among friends. And until then, you suckers will do.
jokes.punchline: Immediately insults them as placeholders until he finds real friends
jokes.explanation: Good Packer character moment showing his inability to be genuinely nice

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: You guys have been great, and I want you to know how much I appreciate you. You're like... you're like real friends to me.

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: But obviously, I'm still looking for actual friends. You're just placeholders until I find some cool people.

jokes.text: Packer is turning in his car for a desk. He is turning in his condoms for a condominium, although he's probably going to have to use condoms from here on out.
jokes.setup: Michael doing parallel structure about Packer's transition
jokes.punchline: Wordplay on condoms/condominium then undermining it
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay structure but Michael overcomplicated the landing

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Just like a condom, a condominium is something you slip into and then regret for the next eight to ten years.

jokes.text: What don't you understand about the word 'approved'? It seems a couple of you don't know what the word 'approved' means. I have very little patience for stupidity.
jokes.setup: Holly explaining she approved Packer's hire
jokes.punchline: Gets increasingly condescending about a simple word
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Holly's unexpected harsh side

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: I approved his hire. He seemed like a good fit.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: What? Why are you looking at me like that?

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: A 'fit' is a good thing. It means he was qualified.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: No, I know what the word means. It's a common word.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: It means... suitable. Compatible. Do you understand what I'm saying?

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: It's not complicated. A fit. Good fit. He was a good fit for the job.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: I don't know how else to explain this to you.

jokes.text: Sorry we're late. Gabe fell in the shower. Such a klutz. Yeah, and it took the fire department forever to get there.
jokes.punchline: Escalates to needing fire department rescue from shower
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gabe emasculation joke with good escalation structure

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: I'm sorry I'm late. I got stuck in my shower this morning.

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: Stuck? What do you mean, stuck?

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: The door wouldn't open. I was in there for like twenty minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: Did you try the handle?

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Of course I tried the handle, Gabe. I'm not an idiot. I had to call the fire department.

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: The fire department came to your house?

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Yeah, they had to break down the shower door. It was very embarrassing.

jokes.text: And I don't want to say the other one was old, but its I.P. Number was one.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why Erin needed new computer
jokes.punchline: Absurd technical joke about IP addresses
jokes.explanation: Clever technical humor with good economy and originality

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Erin needed a new computer because her IP address was getting too old. I told her, 'Erin, your IP address is like your social security number for the internet.' She said, 'So I need to change it every time I commit identity theft?'

jokes.text: You know, I cleaned up our daughter for, like, an hour at 4:00 A.M. This morning, so... So you know the feeling.
jokes.setup: Pam talking about helping someone feels good
jokes.punchline: Jim's parenting reality check
jokes.explanation: Good married couple banter with parenting reality

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: It feels good to help someone, you know?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah, it does. Until you have kids and realize you're just helping them avoid becoming a burden to society.

jokes.text: We have been thinking about where packer should sit, and we can't just sit around waiting for creed to die.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions waiting for Creed to die
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Michael humor with great economy and shock value

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm just waiting for Creed to die so I can have his desk. It's the best spot in the office.

jokes.text: 'Packer was here and so was your mom.'
jokes.setup: Dwight claiming his name is carved under desk
jokes.punchline: It's actually Packer's juvenile graffiti
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that subverts Dwight's claim with typical Packer juvenile humor

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: My name is carved under this desk.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's not your name, Dwight. That's Packer's juvenile graffiti.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: What? No, I carved that myself!

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: It says 'Dwight is a dork.' That's Packer.

jokes.text: It's $20 to watch, Halpert.
jokes.punchline: Packer treating it like entertainment to charge for
jokes.explanation: Typical Packer crass behavior but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: You want your desk back? That'll be fifty bucks.

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: What? I'm running a business here. Supply and demand, baby.

jokes.text: Whose dirt box is this? That's our zen garden. What do you grow in here, bull crap?
jokes.punchline: Crude joke about what grows in dirt
jokes.explanation: Predictable crude humor that fits Packer's character

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: Hey, what's with the zen garden? You trying to find inner peace or something?

dialogue.speaker: Office worker
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's supposed to be calming.

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: Well, you know what else grows in dirt? My sense of humor.

jokes.text: You think you're so cute with your pretty blond hair.
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at sarcastic insult that sounds like compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela moment - trying to be mean but being ineffective

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Well, Holly, I have to say... your incompetence is almost impressive. Almost.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: Thank you?

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: That wasn't a compliment.

jokes.text: That's how he gets you to take off your panties. - Why are you nodding? - United front.
jokes.punchline: Phyllis nodding along then explaining 'united front'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Phyllis's unexpected solidarity

dialogue.speaker: Meredith
dialogue.line: He's like a bad rash that keeps coming back.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Exactly. We need to present a united front against him.

jokes.text: Right. This isn't the U.S. government. What are you referencing? Everything. Everything.
jokes.punchline: Ryan's vague pseudo-intellectual response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan character moment - pretentious but empty commentary

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: You know, the government is like a big machine, and we're all just tiny parts.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: That's actually really deep when you think about it.

jokes.text: Well, I mean, he humped Michael. Well, if that's the case, I guess I've got to be fired too.
jokes.punchline: Holly implying she also humped Michael
jokes.explanation: Good sexual humor with Holly's unexpected directness

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Packer is such a Neanderthal. He humped my leg like a dog.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, he humped mine too.

jokes.text: This computer's a hunk of junk. Hey, sailor, come watch your regatta over here.
jokes.setup: Gabe complaining about computer during sailing video
jokes.punchline: Andy's sailing-themed invitation using nautical terminology
jokes.explanation: Good Andy character moment showing his preppy sailing obsession

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: This computer is moving too slow. I need it to go faster.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Have you tried turning it off and on again?

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: I'm not an idiot.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I'm throwing a sailing party. You're invited. All hands on deck.

jokes.text: This is a sick computer. Gwen Stefani has this computer.
jokes.punchline: Random celebrity reference as evidence of quality
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy try-hard moment but reference feels random

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Erin, this new computer is top-of-the-line. It's so fast, it makes the MacBook Air look like a MacBook Turtle.

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: A MacBook Turtle?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's slow. Like a turtle. It's a funny comparison because turtles are slow and so would be a slow computer named after a fast animal.

jokes.text: Yeah, but if you donated my computer to Africa, it would become famous as the slowest computer in Africa.
jokes.setup: Andy complaining about his computer speed
jokes.punchline: Hyperbolic comparison to entire continent
jokes.explanation: Good hyperbolic structure with solid Andy whining energy

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: My computer is so slow, it's like trying to download the entire continent of Africa on a 56k modem.

jokes.text: So something good happening to Stanley is crazy now.
jokes.setup: Pam saying getting everyone computers would be crazy
jokes.punchline: Stanley's dry interpretation as personal slight
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley deadpan delivery with great economy and character integration

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Getting everyone computers would be crazy.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: So you're saying I'm not worth a computer.

jokes.text: What, are you gonna play mah-jongg faster?
jokes.punchline: Andy stereotyping what older women do on computers
jokes.explanation: Mildly ageist humor that fits Andy's insensitivity but not clever

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: I want a new computer.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Why? So you can email chain letters and look up pictures of cats?

jokes.text: Yeah. Three muske-queers.
jokes.punchline: Homophobic pun on Three Musketeers
jokes.explanation: Fits Packer's character but relies on offensive humor without cleverness

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: All for one and one for all? More like all for one and none for the ladies!

jokes.text: I don't know. I love her and all, but she's turned into a bitch. Most days she's great, but some days she acts like her mom.
jokes.setup: Asked about spending time with his daughter
jokes.punchline: Calls his daughter a bitch for acting like her mother
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable character moment showing Packer's toxicity as parent

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: I'd love to spend more time with my daughter, but she's been acting like such a bitch lately. I mean, she's turning into her mother!

jokes.text: Since when did you learn how to read? I do know how to read, though. Yeah. You know how to read a menu.
jokes.setup: Kevin suggesting Packer write a book
jokes.punchline: Packer mocking Kevin's literacy then focusing on food
jokes.explanation: Typical Packer bullying behavior targeting Kevin's perceived intelligence

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: You should write a book, Todd. You've got so many great stories.

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: A book? Kevin, I'd need you to read it to me first.

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: Besides, I'm not really a writer. Now, a cookbook? That I could do. 'Todd Packer's Guide to Eating Meat and Telling Inappropriate Stories.'

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: That actually sounds pretty good.

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: Of course it does. It's got meat and stories. What else does anyone need?

jokes.text: Kevin, in sumo culture, you'd be considered a promising up-and-comer.
jokes.setup: Kevin feeling bad about weight comments
jokes.punchline: Dwight's attempt at comfort through cultural reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - genuine attempt to help through weird cultural knowledge

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Kevin, you are a beautiful person. On the inside and the outside. You remind me of a young Orson Welles.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Thanks, Dwight.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: He was also a great man. Great man.

jokes.text: Erin just wanted me to have it, so we switched. What? Erin, is that true?
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why he has Erin's computer
jokes.punchline: Pam's disbelief at this obvious manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good setup for workplace manipulation but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Erin asked me to hold onto her computer while she was out.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Andy, she's sitting right there.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I know, but she asked me to hold it.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: She's looking right at you.

jokes.text: When I'm freaking out, I just sort of step back, take three deep breaths. I'm not freaking out. Okay? And then I ask myself, 'is this worth freaking out about?'
jokes.punchline: Clearly is freaking out while denying it
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment showing Pam's attempt to maintain control

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: I'm totally fine. I'm calm. Everything is fine.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: I use this breathing technique where I just breathe in and out, in and out, and it keeps me centered.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: I'm fine!

jokes.text: Andy, why should she have your crappy computer? Oh, that's interesting. So you also think my computer's crappy.
jokes.punchline: Andy takes it as confirmation his computer sucks
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Andy's defensive nature

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Your computer doesn't suck, Andy. Erin just has a really fast workflow.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So you're saying my computer IS slow.

jokes.text: Please make sure nobody is humping me.
jokes.setup: Andy going to switch computers back
jokes.punchline: Paranoid request about being humped
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier Packer behavior with Andy's paranoia

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Hey, when I switch my computer back, can you promise me you won't hump it?

jokes.text: Best day ever. Best day ever. So much is happening.
jokes.punchline: Erin's genuine excitement about drama
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin character moment - finding joy in workplace chaos

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Oh my God, this is so exciting! I love when things happen!

jokes.text: Question... should I get stripes shaved into the side of my head? No. No. Please.
jokes.punchline: Pam's desperate pleading response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Erin's questionable style choices

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Pam, what do you think? Should I wear the yellow or the pink?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Oh, um... I think... either one is great, Erin.

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: But which one?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Please, Erin, I'm begging you. Just pick one and go. Pick one and go!

jokes.text: Oh, he's funnier than me. He's not funny at all. So I'm less funny than not funny at all?
jokes.punchline: Realizes he just called himself not funny
jokes.explanation: Good Michael self-deprecating moment with logical progression

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm nothing like Packer. I'm funny, I'm charming, I'm a great boss...

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wait, did I just call myself not funny?

jokes.text: Okay, bill cosby, Steve Martin, Charlie bit my finger, Michael Scott, then all the way down here, Todd packer.
jokes.punchline: Mixing viral video with comedy legends and putting Michael above Packer
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy hierarchy with unexpected inclusion of viral video

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: So you're saying viral videos are funnier than Charlie Chaplin?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm not saying that. I'm just saying that if you had to pick between watching a Charlie Chaplin film or watching a guy get hit in the nuts with a football, most people are going to choose the guy getting hit in the nuts.

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: That's ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, it's not. It's funny because it's unexpected. Charlie Chaplin, you know what you're getting. Sad clown.

jokes.text: 'Cause I want to let bygones be bygones, show you I'm cool. You're the new guy. It's cold out. I made too much. That's a lot of reasons.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining why he made Packer hot chocolate
jokes.punchline: Packer noting the suspicious number of reasons
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jim overselling his fake kindness

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I made you hot chocolate because it's cold outside, you looked tired, and I thought you'd appreciate it.

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: That's three reasons, Jim. Three. You're making this real suspicious.

jokes.text: The only hot chocolate I'm into is Vivica A. Fox.
jokes.punchline: Sexual reference to Black actress
jokes.explanation: Typical Packer crude humor with racial undertones, fits character but not clever

jokes.text: It isn't poison. It's a laxative. People take laxatives all the time. This is just a lot more of a laxative.
jokes.setup: Jim questioning Dwight's hot chocolate plan
jokes.punchline: Dwight's disturbing justification
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight logic showing his dangerous problem-solving approach

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Why would you put a whole egg in hot chocolate?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: It's a medieval recipe. The yolk creates a creamier texture, and the white prevents scurvy.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Eggs don't prevent scurvy.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Not with that attitude.

jokes.text: Does Edgar Allen poe know about that one? So sinister.
jokes.punchline: Jim sarcastically comparing it to gothic horror writer
jokes.explanation: Excellent literary reference showing Jim's wit and sarcasm about mild prank

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: We could jam his drawers. That would really get him going.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah, or we could just read him passages from Edgar Allan Poe. Really set the mood.

jokes.text: Who is justice beaver? He... it's a crime-fighting beaver.
jokes.punchline: Creates elaborate backstory for misheard name
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dwight moment with perfect structure and instant quotability. Shows his earnest creativity.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I was listening to the radio and heard this new artist. Justin... Beaver.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: It's Bieber.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's what I said. Justin Beaver. He's a former Olympic swimmer from Canada who transitioned into music after a tragic poolside incident left him unable to compete.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: No, Dwight—

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: His parents were beavers. Literal beavers. That's where he gets his stage name. Very poignant.

jokes.text: I put a sign-up sheet in the conference room, and I signed us up for three mods. A mod is five minutes, and it started two minutes ago, so...
jokes.punchline: Overcomplicated bureaucratic solution with made-up terminology
jokes.explanation: Good Jim moment showing his elaborate solutions to simple problems

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Hey, can we talk privately for a second?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Of course. We'll need to file a Form 27-B: Request for Confidential Discourse, submit it to corporate, wait for approval, then schedule the conversation during the designated inter-departmental dialogue window.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Or we could just... go in the break room?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That would bypass proper channels. Highly irregular.

jokes.text: Well, if it breaks all the way, I can get you a new one. Pretty sneaky, sis.
jokes.punchline: Andy's creepy interpretation and pet name
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Andy misreading social cues

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: So the company replaces your computer every five years.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Every five years? That's like dog years for technology.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Yes, exactly.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So my computer is my little puppy dog. I'm gonna call it Precious.

jokes.text: There are 400 of these. Yeah, I couldn't cut it down. They're all good.
jokes.setup: Jim asking for Dwight's top 40 prank ideas
jokes.punchline: Dwight delivers 400 and thinks they're all perfect
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Dwight's inability to edit himself

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Dwight, I need your top 40 prank ideas.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I have 400. And they're all perfect.

jokes.text: 'Number three... eat a frog.' That sounds promising. 'Number four... eat a dog.'
jokes.punchline: Ideas escalate from weird to disturbing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Dwight's disturbing creativity

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Okay, so Dwight's got a whole notebook of prank ideas here. Let's see... 'Put a fake spider in his desk.' That's pretty standard. 'Replace his stapler with a lighter.' Okay, getting creative. 'Convince him that he's been in a coma for three years.' Alright, that's dark. 'Dress up as his dead mother and sit in his chair.' Whoa, whoa, whoa. Why would you— I'm burning this notebook.

jokes.text: 'Eat a brog.' Maybe it's 'cause I didn't understand it.
jokes.punchline: Jim's polite confusion about made-up word
jokes.explanation: Good Jim deadpan response to Dwight's nonsense with great economy

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, what?

jokes.text: Allow all cookies? Why, 'soitenly.' Pop-ups? Yes, please. Bit torrent streaming from a Somalian music website? Yeah, why not?
jokes.punchline: Escalating approvals ending with dangerous foreign site
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure showing Andy's petty sabotage getting extreme

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay, so I've disabled all the safe sites. Now she has to go through approval chains.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: First she needs my sign-off, then Dwight's, then corporate compliance.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: And if she somehow gets past all that, the final approval comes from a server in Belarus.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Which, let's be honest, is basically just a guy named Sergei who approves everything for five dollars.

jokes.text: Oh, I hope you don't get sick, Mr. computer. Why are you doing this to me, Andy?
jokes.setup: Andy's fake concern for computer he's sabotaging
jokes.punchline: Erin's direct question exposing his obvious manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Erin cutting through Andy's facade

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Oh no, this computer's been acting up. I'm so worried about it. Maybe we should just get a new one?

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Andy, you literally just poured coffee on it.

jokes.text: Michael, can I open the kimono with you? I've been on the road too long.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate idiom usage
jokes.explanation: Typical Packer inappropriate language but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: Look, I want to have an honest conversation with you. No games, no BS.

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: I think we need to clear the air and really get down to brass tax here.

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: I mean, we've been dancing around this for too long. Time to bite the bullet and let the cat out of the bag.

jokes.text: I'm sorry if you were offended by my comments earlier. Well, like I said, I wasn't offended, but I'll stand here for the big show.
jokes.punchline: Kevin sees through it and plays along sarcastically
jokes.explanation: Good moment showing Kevin's unexpected awareness of social dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: Kevin, I want to apologize for everything I've done. I've been a real jerk, and you deserve better than that.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Oh, that's so nice of you to say, Todd. I really appreciate that. Does this mean you're buying lunch for everyone today?

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: Well, I wouldn't go that far...

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Yeah, I didn't think so.

jokes.text: Kevin, I am so sorry. You are skinny, and you're a genius. That... that was maybe too much.
jokes.punchline: Obviously false compliments then admitting he overdid it
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing Packer's inability to give genuine compliments

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: Hey, I wanted to apologize. You're a great guy, really talented, great sense of humor, incredible person.

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm overdoing it, aren't I?

jokes.text: Packer is a survivor of divorce, Stanley. Packer doesn't speak with his child, Meredith. Packer never lived up to his parents' expectations, Andy.
jokes.punchline: Each defense makes Packer sound worse
jokes.explanation: Excellent Michael moment where his attempt to help makes things worse

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, look, Packer's not a bad guy. He's just... he's a guy who likes to have fun.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And yes, maybe some of that fun involves situations that are legally questionable, but that doesn't make him a bad person.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: He's very loyal. I mean, he's loyal to himself, which is the most important kind of loyalty.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And sure, he may have done some things that hurt people, but he felt bad about it. For like, seconds.

jokes.text: Angela loves pussycats. Packers loves... - No, don't. - I was going to say 'dogs'!
jokes.punchline: Everyone assumes sexual reference but he claims innocence
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Michael's typical obliviousness to double entendres

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Angela, you and I are a lot alike.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: We are nothing alike.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, no, we are. We're both very rigid, very controlling, we have very high standards.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: That's not—

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And we're both very difficult to get into.

jokes.text: Now I know why jo has kept her eye on you. And you make sure to get down there and check out the Harry Potter world.
jokes.setup: Michael giving Packer fake job details
jokes.punchline: Mixing business with tourist attractions
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt at deception mixing legitimate and tourist advice

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, so here's the deal with your new position. You'll be based out of our Scranton branch, but you'll also be spending a lot of time at Steamtown Mall.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: It's a real hands-on role. Very experiential. You'll be learning the ins and outs of retail, tourism, and soft pretzels.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Plus, there's a carousel right there, so you can take breaks whenever you need to. The company pays for your carousel rides.

jokes.text: I'm a huge alligator nerd. I can name you every genus, every subspecies. Also, I'm a huge boob nerd.
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals crude interest
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing Packer's inability to maintain intellectual facade

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: You know, I've been reading a lot lately. Really getting into philosophy and literature.

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: Yeah, I just finished this incredible book about the human condition.

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: It had this one scene with a woman bending over to pick something up...

jokes.text: All I'm saying is, in about a month or so, have you down to Florida, introduce you to all the local talent.
jokes.setup: Packer talking to Michael about Florida
jokes.punchline: Reducing women to 'local talent' like a commodity
jokes.explanation: Typical Packer objectification of women, fits character but not clever

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: I'm telling you, Michael, Florida is amazing. The weather, the beaches, and the local talent.

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: Local talent?

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: Yeah, you know what I mean. The women down there are incredible.

jokes.text: I'm sorry about your friend. No. He's an ass. You are. What are you, wicked smaht? No, you are.
jokes.punchline: Michael's 'you are' battle with Boston accent
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay with regional accent humor but not particularly strong

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: He's really awful.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You ah!

dialogue.speaker: Holly
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You ah awful. I'm from Boston.

jokes.text: Yeah, you really did, 'cause I know every inch of that warehouse. Yep. Super lucky.
jokes.setup: Darryl questioning Pam's warehouse computer find
jokes.punchline: Pam's obvious lying under pressure
jokes.explanation: Good tension moment showing Pam caught in her deception

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: So you're telling me you found a computer in the warehouse?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Yes. It was just... sitting there.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Pam, we've worked here for years. There's no computer in the warehouse.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Well, maybe it was... a really good hiding spot?

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: You're sweating.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: It's warm in here!

jokes.text: Maybe you could go back down there and see if you can find me some extra sick days. Yeah. You know what? I think I saw one sick day... I think maybe I saw five. Three.
jokes.setup: Darryl sarcastically asking for sick days from warehouse
jokes.punchline: Pam's negotiation showing her corruption
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Pam's character evolution into corruption

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Yeah, I need to take some sick days.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: How many?

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Five.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Okay, I can do three. But you have to owe me one.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Owe you one what?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: A favor. Any favor I want. No questions asked.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: That's extortion.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: That's negotiation.

jokes.text: I'm full on corrupt.
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact admission of complete corruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery with great economy showing Pam's character growth

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: I've done things that would make a mob boss blush. I've stolen office supplies, lied on timesheets, and once I may have embezzled... no, definitely embezzled. I'm basically a criminal now.

tmdb_overview: The office holds a garage sale in the warehouse. Michael decides to propose to Holly, and runs into trouble thinking of how to do it well.
insight: Mild cringe carries a below-average episode through character comedy, not joke density.
